text.title: Backup Verification + Disaster Recovery Drills Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the backup-verification-dr methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every backup pipeline MUST be exercised by an automated restore drill at least weekly; the drill MUST restore to a fresh disposable target and run a verification query (row-count, checksum, smoke test).

rationale.source: DORA Reliability research, Internal DR drill program

rule.rationale: Backups never tested at restore time fail at restore time; the >50% failure rate is repeatedly measured across audit programs.

rule.statement: Backup age MUST be observable as a Prometheus metric (`backup_age_seconds`) and alert when it exceeds 1.5x the expected interval.

rationale.source: Prometheus best practices, SRE Workbook ch.4

rule.rationale: An invisible stalled cron leaves the team certain backups are running while the file in S3 ages for weeks.

rule.statement: Backup size MUST be observable as a Prometheus metric (`backup_size_bytes`); alert on > 50% drop vs 7-day median.
rule.rationale: Sudden size collapse indicates the dump truncated, the source pruned data, or the script lost a flag.

rule.statement: DR procedure MUST be a versioned runbook (Markdown with tagged steps) the agent can execute; ad-hoc tribal-knowledge restore is forbidden.
rule.rationale: On-call without a runbook produces non-deterministic restore times and risks data loss from one-off shell improvisation.

rule.statement: Each drill MUST emit `dr_rpo_seconds_actual` and `dr_rto_seconds_actual` metrics; alert when actual > 1.2x target.
rule.rationale: Without measured RPO/RTO the team can claim any objective; ground truth is the drill timer.

rule.statement: If no backup pipeline is in place yet, this methodology MUST be skipped — produce the backup pipeline first (`backup-database-postgres`, `backup-filesystem-restic`, etc.).
rule.rationale: Verification methodology applied to no backups is theatre.
